content.problemStatement:
There is a programming contest held by ShareChat. According to the contest rules, submissions do not receive any feedback until the end of the contest. The judges wanted to benefit from this fact to make their favourite contestants win the contest.

In one of the problems, there were a total of NNN submissions (numbered 111 through NNN) received. The judges prepared a total of MMM tests (numbered 111 through MMM) for this problem. For each submission iii and each test jjj, we know that the time this submission ran on this test is Ai,jA_{i, j}Ai,j​ and whether it produced a correct answer or not.

The judges can still decide the time limit for this problem. For each submission, they have decided whether they wanted it to pass all the tests or not. You are given an integer sequence D1,D2,…,DND_1, D_2, \dots, D_ND1​,D2​,…,DN​; Di=1D_i = 1Di​=1 denotes that submission iii should pass all tests and Di=0D_i = 0Di​=0 denotes that it should fail on at least one test, either by exceeding the time limit (getting TLE) or producing an incorrect answer (getting WA).

Now, the judges want to decide the time limit of the problem and the subset of tests they should use so that each submission gets the desired verdict (passes or fails).
constraints: The sum of N for all test cases does not exceed 1000
The sum of M for all test cases does not exceed 1000
1 ≤ Ai,j ≤ 10^8 for each valid i, j
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers N and M. The second line contains N space-separated integers D1, D2, …, DN. N lines follow. For each valid i, the i-th of these lines contains M space-separated integers Ai,1, Ai,2, …, Ai,M. N more lines follow. For each valid i, the i-th of these lines contains M space-separated integers Bi,1, Bi,2, …, Bi,M.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a line containing the string "NO" if it is impossible to find a solution or "YES" otherwise. If a solution exists, print two more lines. The first of these lines should contain two space-separated integers T (the time limit, 1 ≤ T ≤ 10^9) and K (the number of tests chosen). The last line should contain K space-separated integers denoting the indices of the chosen tests.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The first test case has 3 submissions and 4 tests. The submissions should pass all tests. The time limit is 10 and the chosen tests are 2 and 3. The second test case has 2 submissions and 2 tests. The first submission should pass and the second submission should fail. However, it is impossible to find a solution.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a graph theory approach. We can create a bipartite graph where the left side represents the submissions and the right side represents the tests. We can add an edge between a submission and a test if the submission passes the test. Then, we can use a matching algorithm to find the maximum matching in the graph. If the size of the matching is equal to the number of submissions that should pass, then we can find a solution. Otherwise, we can print "NO".
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * (N * M + N * M)) — The time complexity is dominated by the iteration over the test cases and the construction of the graph.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(T * (N + M)) — The space complexity is dominated by the storage of the graph.
